metadata.title: Brief an Stefan Zweig vom 21. Mai 1940, SZ-AAP/B3.17

context:
## Dieses Dokument

- Titel: Brief an Stefan Zweig vom 21. Mai 1940, SZ-AAP/B3.17
- Signatur: SZ-AAP/B3.17
- Sprache: Englisch
- Objekttyp: Brief
- Umfang: 3 Scans

pages.transcription:
THE INTERNATIONAL COPYRIGHT BUREAU LTD
(FOUNDED BY ERNEST MAYER.)

REPRESENTING
DRAMATISTS COMPOSERS STAGE & FILM PRODUCERS MUSIC PUBLISHERS

TELEGRAMS
VOLSCIUS LONDON

DEWAR HOUSE
HAYMARKET
LONDON S.W.1

May 21st, 1940.

Dr.Stefan Zweig,
Lyncombe Hill,
Bath.

Dear Dr.Zweig,

Thank you for your letter of the 16th inst.- We have written
to Dr.Horch as per our draft, and hope to hear from him in due course,when
we will, of course,communicate with you again.

We have to approach you to-day in a rather unpleasant matter,
in connection with the "VOLPONE" film rights. You will remember that we
discussed this matter with you last summer,and that you advised us to make
it clear to the Theatre Guild that there were two different versions of
"VOLPONE" in existence: the one you wrote in collaboration with Jules Romains
and which was eventually sold for a film ein France, and the other version
written by you alone, and acquired and performed by the Theatre Guild. This
we have done, and have recently sent copy of your letter to the Theatre Guild,
as they had had trouble with their client,Mr.Hakim,who had acquired an option
on the English film rights,and now asks for the return of the money beacause
the French film of "Volpone" is being announced by the Ile de France Film Co.

We are enclosing copy of our reply to the Theatre Guild,which
we hope,will find your approval. We must,however,leave it to you to get in
touch with the "Ile de France" Co.in this matter,as we have had nothing to
do with the sale of the French film rights,and have no details available.

Perhaps you will be good enough to let us know what attitude
you wish to take,and let us have copy of any letter you may intend to write
to the Theatre Guild on the matter. We would in fact appreciate it if you
would write to the Theatre Guild via this firm,so that we can be fully in-
formed of any steps that are being taken.

With kindest regards,

Yours sincerely,
F of the letter received from the Theatre
Guild to-day,and copy ...
ENCL:
IMPORTANT.-Authors are kindly requested to note that The International Copyright Bureau Ltd. only accept MSS. for negotiation
on the understanding that they shall not be held responsible for their accidental loss by fire or any other cause whilst in their offices
in transit or on offer to Clients or prospective Clients to whom they may have been submitted in the ordinary course of business.
pages.notes: Das Dokument ist ein maschinengeschriebener Geschäftsbrief. Die handschriftlichen Ergänzungen am Ende (vor 'ENCL:') scheinen eine Notiz des Empfängers oder des Büros zu sein. Die Scans 2 und 3 zeigen lediglich die Rückseite bzw. eine mit Farbkarten überlagerte Version des gleichen Dokuments.

result.confidence_notes: Der Text ist ein klar lesbarer, maschinengeschriebener Geschäftsbrief. Die handschriftlichen Zusätze am Ende sind ebenfalls gut lesbar.
